SEARCH
Package be.ac.umons.frontend

Class WindowingController

java.lang.Object
be.ac.umons.frontend.WindowingController
public class WindowingController extends Object
Controls the user interface for visualizing axis-aligned segments and applying windowing queries. Handles file loading, segment rendering on a canvas, and query window display.

  • Constructor Details

    • WindowingController

      public WindowingController(javafx.stage.Stage stage)
      Creates a controller bound to the given JavaFX stage.
      Parameters:
      stage - the primary stage used for file dialogs

  • Method Details

    • buildUI

      public javafx.scene.layout.BorderPane buildUI()
      Builds and returns the root layout of the user interface. The layout includes a control panel on the left with load, display, and query controls, and a scrollable canvas on the right for rendering segments.
      Returns:
      the root BorderPane of the interface

    • loadFile

      private void loadFile()
      Opens a file chooser dialog and loads segments from the selected file. On success, initializes the windowing engine and displays all segments. On failure, shows an error dialog.

    • displaySegments

      private void displaySegments(List<Segment> segments, Window queryWindow)

    • displaySegments

      private void displaySegments(List<Segment> horizontalSegments, List<Segment> verticalSegments, Window queryWindow)
      Renders the given horizontal and vertical segments on the canvas. If a query window is provided, it is drawn as a highlighted rectangle over the canvas.
      Parameters:
      horizontalSegments - the horizontal segments to draw
      verticalSegments - the vertical segments to draw
      queryWindow - the query window to highlight, or null to skip

    • applyWindowing

      private void applyWindowing()
      Reads the query bounds from the input fields, runs the windowing query, and displays the resulting segments along with the query window. Shows an error dialog if the window bounds are invalid.

    • toScreenX

      private double toScreenX(double x, Window bw, double offsetX, double scale)
      Converts a world x coordinate to a screen x coordinate.
      Parameters:
      x - the world x coordinate
      bw - the bounding window used for scaling
      offsetX - the horizontal canvas offset
      scale - the scale factor
      Returns:
      the screen x coordinate

    • toScreenY

      private double toScreenY(double y, Window bw, double offsetY, double scale)
      Converts a world y coordinate to a screen y coordinate. The y axis is flipped so that higher world values appear higher on screen.
      Parameters:
      y - the world y coordinate
      bw - the bounding window used for scaling
      offsetY - the vertical canvas offset
      scale - the scale factor
      Returns:
      the screen y coordinate

    • parseOrDefault

      private double parseOrDefault(String text, double def)
      Parses a string as a double, returning a default value if the string is null or blank.
      Parameters:
      text - the string to parse
      def - the default value to return if parsing is not possible
      Returns:
      the parsed value, or def if the string is null or blank

    • drawSegment

      private void drawSegment(javafx.scene.canvas.GraphicsContext gc, Window bw, double offsetX, double offsetY, Point p1, Point p2, double scale)
      Draws a single segment on the canvas by converting both endpoints to screen coordinates.
      Parameters:
      gc - the graphics context to draw on
      bw - the bounding window used for scaling
      offsetX - the horizontal canvas offset
      offsetY - the vertical canvas offset
      p1 - the first endpoint of the segment
      p2 - the second endpoint of the segment
      scale - the scale factor

    • showError

      private void showError(String title, String msg)
      Displays an error dialog with the given title and message.
      Parameters:
      title - the dialog title
      msg - the error message
